name: Frank-ly Paella, Barcelona Style
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Tomato, peeled, seeded and diced
1 Green Bell Pepper, seeded and diced
1 Red Bell Pepper, seeded and cut into thin strips or 1 jar whole pimento, drained and cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
2. Cut four franks into quarters lengthwise. Slice remaining franks into 3 pieces.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a large skillet. Add frank strips and pieces and cook until lightly browned; remove and set aside.
4. Add another tablespoon of olive oil and cocktail franks and salami slices. Lightly brown; remove and set aside.
5. Add remaining olive oil, garlic, and onion to the skillet and cook, stirring for 3 minutes.
6. Add rice and saffron. Cook and stir until rice turns golden.
7. Add chicken broth and bay leaf. Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
8. In a shallow 4-quart oven-proof casserole, arrange rice, sausages, tomato, and bell peppers so that some of each show on top.
9. Heat in the oven for 15 to 20 minutes. Yield: 6 to 8 servings.
